question: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. A pair of co-interior angles are $(4x + 15)^\circ$ and $(5x + 12)^\circ$. Find the size of the larger angle.
explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines are supplementary. $4x + 15 + 5x + 12 = 180 \implies 9x = 153 \implies x = 17$. The angles are $83^\circ$ and $97^\circ$, so the larger is $97^\circ$.

question: In an isosceles triangle, the two equal base angles each measure $(1x + 15)^\circ$. The vertex angle measures $(1x + 60)^\circ$. Find the value of $x$.
explanation: Sum of angles is $180^\circ$. $2(1x + 15) + (1x + 60) = 180 \implies 3x + 90 = 180 \implies 3x = 90 \implies x = 30$.

explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines are supplementary. $3x + 19 + 5x + 17 = 180 \implies 8x = 144 \implies x = 18$. The angles are $73^\circ$ and $107^\circ$, so the larger is $107^\circ$.

question: Angles subtended by the same arc in a circle measure $(3x + 6)^\circ$ and $(3x + 6)^\circ$. Determine the size of the angle.
explanation: Angles in the same segment are equal. $3x + 6 = 3x + 6 \implies 0x = 0 \implies x = 17$. The angle is $3(17) + 6 = 57^\circ$.

question: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. A pair of co-interior angles are $(4x + 10)^\circ$ and $(6x + 20)^\circ$. Find the size of the larger angle.

question: Angles subtended by the same arc in a circle measure $(3x + 11)^\circ$ and $(3x + 11)^\circ$. Determine the size of the angle.

question: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. A pair of co-interior angles are $(2x + 14)^\circ$ and $(3x + 6)^\circ$. Find the size of the larger angle.
explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines are supplementary. $2x + 14 + 3x + 6 = 180 \implies 5x = 160 \implies x = 32$. The angles are $78^\circ$ and $102^\circ$, so the larger is $102^\circ$.

question: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. A pair of co-interior angles are $(2x + 15)^\circ$ and $(6x + 13)^\circ$. Find the size of the larger angle.
explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines are supplementary. $2x + 15 + 6x + 13 = 180 \implies 8x = 152 \implies x = 19$. The angles are $53^\circ$ and $127^\circ$, so the larger is $127^\circ$.

question: Two vertically opposite angles measure $(5x + 26)^\circ$ and $(4x +39)^\circ$. Find the value of $x$.
explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal: $5x + 26 = 4x +39 \implies 1x = 13 \implies x = 13$.

question: In an isosceles triangle, the two equal base angles each measure $(2x + 12)^\circ$. The vertex angle measures $(3x + 16)^\circ$. Find the value of $x$.
explanation: Sum of angles is $180^\circ$. $2(2x + 12) + (3x + 16) = 180 \implies 7x + 40 = 180 \implies 7x = 140 \implies x = 20$.

question: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. A pair of co-interior angles are $(3x + 12)^\circ$ and $(3x + 6)^\circ$. Find the size of the larger angle.
explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines are supplementary. $3x + 12 + 3x + 6 = 180 \implies 6x = 162 \implies x = 27$. The angles are $93^\circ$ and $87^\circ$, so the larger is $93^\circ$.

explanation: Sum of angles is $180^\circ$. $2(2x + 14) + (1x + 87) = 180 \implies 5x + 115 = 180 \implies 5x = 65 \implies x = 13$.

question: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. A pair of co-interior angles are $(3x + 7)^\circ$ and $(3x + 17)^\circ$. Find the size of the larger angle.
explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines are supplementary. $3x + 7 + 3x + 17 = 180 \implies 6x = 156 \implies x = 26$. The angles are $85^\circ$ and $95^\circ$, so the larger is $95^\circ$.

question: Two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal. A pair of co-interior angles are $(3x + 17)^\circ$ and $(4x + 9)^\circ$. Find the size of the larger angle.
explanation: Co-interior angles between parallel lines are supplementary. $3x + 17 + 4x + 9 = 180 \implies 7x = 154 \implies x = 22$. The angles are $83^\circ$ and $97^\circ$, so the larger is $97^\circ$.

</svg>

question: In an isosceles triangle, the two equal base angles each measure $(3x + 7)^\circ$. The vertex angle measures $(2x + 38)^\circ$. Find the value of $x$.
explanation: Sum of angles is $180^\circ$. $2(3x + 7) + (2x + 38) = 180 \implies 8x + 52 = 180 \implies 8x = 128 \implies x = 16$.

question: In an isosceles triangle, the two equal base angles each measure $(1x + 14)^\circ$. The vertex angle measures $(1x + 110)^\circ$. Find the value of $x$.

question: Angles subtended by the same arc in a circle measure $(3x + 10)^\circ$ and $(3x + 10)^\circ$. Determine the size of the angle.
explanation: Angles in the same segment are equal. $3x + 10 = 3x + 10 \implies 0x = 0 \implies x = 19$. The angle is $3(19) + 10 = 67^\circ$.
